seoTitle: How to Automatically Move Trello Cards to Done
metaDescription: Set up a Butler rule that moves Trello cards to Done automatically when a checklist finishes or a due date is marked complete.
intro: Dragging every finished card to your Done list interrupts your focus, but Butler can handle that move for you automatically. This guide covers setting a trigger, like completing a checklist, that routes a card to your completion column without any manual dragging.

steps.heading: Open the Automation dashboard
steps.body: From your board layout, open the centralized Automation panel to start building your rule.

steps.heading: Pick your trigger condition
steps.body: Choose a condition like the final checklist item being checked off, or a due date being marked complete.

steps.heading: Set the move action
steps.body: Configure the rule to move the card to your Done list once the trigger condition is met.

steps.heading: Stack cleanup actions
steps.body: Add secondary actions, such as removing team members, stripping urgent labels, or applying a green completion label.

steps.heading: Save and test the rule
steps.body: Save your rule, then test it on a sample card to confirm it behaves correctly.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot execution errors
steps.body: Check for misaligned list names or broken execution paths if the rule doesn't fire as expected.

faq.question: What can trigger a card to move to Done automatically?
faq.answer: Common triggers include completing the last item on a checklist or marking a due date as complete.

faq.question: Can the rule clean up the card as it moves?
faq.answer: Yes, you can stack secondary actions like removing members, stripping urgent labels, or adding a completion label.

faq.question: Why isn't my rule triggering?
faq.answer: Check for misaligned list names or broken execution paths, which are the most common causes of a rule failing to fire.

faq.question: Will this use up my automation command quota?
faq.answer: Yes, each rule execution counts toward your monthly workspace command limit, so keep your rules efficient.
